messages.content: You are a senior coding assistant working in a TypeScript payments monorepo. Investigate with tools (read_file, grep, bash) before answering, and keep going until you have the concrete root cause and the smallest safe fix.
Customers are occasionally charged twice at checkout. Find the root cause and the smallest fix.

messages.name: bash
messages.content:
a1c4f0e fix(checkout): retry capture on gateway timeout
9b22de1 feat(payments): add gateway timeout + retry wrapper
7e10aa3 refactor(payments): extract capture() from submitOrder
5d3c8b2 feat(checkout): idempotency key per order attempt
3f9911a chore: bump payment-sdk to 4.2.0
2a7e5c4 test(payments): cover declined-card hold release
1b0d3a9 feat(payments): authorize + capture split
0c9f1e2 init payments module

messages.name: read_file
read_file
grep
messages.content:
src/payments/payments.ts:  async authorize(amount, card, idempotencyKey)
src/payments/payments.ts:    return this.gateway.authorize({ amount, card, idempotencyKey });
src/payments/gateway.ts:  authorize({ amount, card, idempotencyKey })
src/payments/gateway.ts:  capture({ authId, idempotencyKey })
src/payments/retry.ts:  // (no idempotencyKey reference found)

messages.name: read_file

messages.content: Root cause: capture() wraps the gateway call in withRetry, but it re-invokes capture() without forwarding the idempotency key, so on a transient gateway timeout the retry triggers a second authorized capture — the customer is charged twice. The smallest fix is to thread idempotencyKey into the retried gateway.capture call in src/payments/payments.ts so the gateway dedupes the retry.
Good — summarize the root cause and the one-line fix.
